justification: As the operator, A is more useful. It hits the exact specialist set the question demands (menu_pricing, review_analyst, marketing_digital), and that shows up in the substance: a sourced platform-channel read (Wolt vs Pyszne.pl, HAOS's 11-13% delivery markup, MIWO's direct-order play) plus a 6% response-rate finding I can act on Monday. Pricing claims are concrete and quote real reviews verbatim, tying value perception to execution inconsistency rather than absolute price. B trades marketing_digital for revenue_sales, and it shows: estimated avg-check chart contradicts its own menu data (Restauracja Moon ranked highest spend at 105 PLN despite mains cheaper than Monsun's). B does land one sharper actionable insight - the Mon/Tue closed, 1 PM open, 8 PM close hours gap vs HAOS - but the internal inconsistency and softer sourcing don't outweigh A's tighter, better-cited operator brief.
